howItWorks.intro: This allergy severity score calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: TNSS = Sneezing + Congestion + Runny Nose + Itchy Eyes + Itchy Throat (0-3 each)
Quality of Life Score = Sleep Impact + Activity Impact (0-3 each)
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is the TNSS allergy score?
faq.answer: The Total Nasal Symptom Score (TNSS) rates four nasal symptoms (sneezing, congestion, runny nose, itchy nose) from 0-3 each. It's used in clinical trials and by allergists to track treatment effectiveness.

faq.question: When should I see an allergist?
faq.answer: See an allergist if: symptoms persist more than 2 weeks, over-the-counter medications aren't helping, symptoms affect sleep or daily activities, or you have complications like sinus infections or asthma.

faq.question: How do I track allergy severity over time?
faq.answer: Score your symptoms at the same time daily (morning is typical). Track scores over weeks to identify patterns, triggers, and medication effectiveness. A consistent score drop of 30%+ indicates effective treatment.

faq.question: Is the Allergy Severity Score Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Allergy Severity Score Calculator?
sections.body: The Allergy Severity Score Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your sneezing, nasal congestion, runny nose, you get instant results including total symptom score, severity category, quality of life impact. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting total symptom score right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your sneezing and need to find the right total symptom score. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Sneezing (0=none, 3=severe)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your sneezing — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: nasal congestion, runny nose, itchy/watery eyes, itchy throat/ears, sleep disruption, daily activity impact. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your total symptom score, severity category, quality of life impact. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your sneezing before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both total symptom score and severity category — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to get accurate results.
If you're unsure about your daily activity impact, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
